decks.notes: The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor, Voice of Victory, and Sand Scout, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Guide of Souls and Lazotep Quarry. The deck also aims to control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and countermagic like Daze. The combination of powerful creatures, disruptive spells, and efficient mana base allows the deck to apply pressure and close out games quickly, while also being able to adapt to different matchups with cards like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Static Prison.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back into play. It also utilizes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect its combo pieces. The deck has a mix of control elements with powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with its efficient reanimation strategy while disrupting their game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with creatures using cards like Faithless Looting and Stitcher's Supplier, in order to bring out powerful creatures like Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis and Vengevine. The deck also utilizes cards like Altar of Dementia and Bridge from Below to create a loop of sacrificing creatures for value. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of graveyard recursion, sacrificing creatures, and bringing out powerful threats at a fast pace.

The deck is focused on quickly getting as many spells onto the battlefield as possible, aiming to win the game in one explosive turn. The deck includes cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly, along with combo pieces like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to quickly mill the entire library. The goal is to have Thassa's Oracle enter the battlefield with no cards left in the library, winning the game instantly. The deck includes protection in the form of Pact of Negation and disruption with cards like Cabal Therapy.

The deck aims to quickly combo off by milling the entire library, either through Balustrade Spy or Undercity Informer, and winning with Thassa's Oracle. It utilizes various rituals and mana accelerants to power out its combo pieces as fast as possible while using discards spells like Cabal Therapy to disrupt opponents. The deck also includes ways to protect its combo with Pact of Negation and Chancellor of the Annex. The key strategy revolves around assembling the combo pieces and executing the win condition swiftly before the opponent can establish their game plan.

This combo deck aims to win the game as quickly as possible by casting a large number of spells in a single turn to mill the entire deck, then using a card like Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot if the combo is successful. The deck includes cards like Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide to generate mana quickly, as well as cards like Pact of Negation to protect the combo from disruption. The primary win condition is to use cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire deck and win with Thassa's Oracle.

The deck focuses on creating a prison-like environment for the opponent by utilizing artifacts to control the board and limit their options. Cards like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus make it difficult for the opponent to cast spells efficiently, while Karn, the Great Creator and The One Ring can shut down opposing strategies. The deck also ramps up quickly with Urza lands and Grim Monolith, allowing for powerful plays like casting Ugin, Eye of the Storms or Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Overall, the deck aims to lock down the opponent while setting up powerful win conditions.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and tempo plays. It utilizes powerful cards like Force of Will, Brainstorm, and Swords to Plowshares to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions. The deck also includes card draw and filtering effects, along with versatile threats like Teferi, Time Raveler and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to maintain board presence and generate card advantage. Wasteland and Stifle are used to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Quantum Riddler offers additional card advantage and disruption.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while maintaining aggressive pressure through efficient creatures and spells. It aims to control the game tempo by using powerful disruption tools like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, while also applying pressure with efficient threats like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. The inclusion of Orcish Bowmasters provides additional removal options, further allowing the deck to control the board state. Overall, this deck seeks to maintain a proactive game plan by disrupting the opponent's plays and consistently applying pressure to secure victory.

The main strategy of this deck is to assemble a powerful combo using Mystic Forge and cards like Manifold Key, Basalt Monolith, and Voltaic Key to generate infinite mana and card advantage. By utilizing Karn, the Great Creator and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to tutor for key artifacts and disrupt your opponent's game plan, this deck aims to control the board and overwhelm your opponent with powerful threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. Additionally, cards like The One Ring and Kozilek's Command provide additional utility and flexibility to adapt to different situations.

The main focus of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent early in the game with aggressive creatures like Barrowgoyf and Nethergoyf, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck also utilizes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Dauthi Voidwalker to gain card advantage and control the board. With a mix of efficient removal spells and powerful creatures, Mono Black Aggro aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to establish control over the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By using cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Prismatic Ending, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage through cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck also utilizes powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Lórien Revealed to close out games once control has been established. The deck's flexibility and efficiency in handling various threats make it a formidable force in the Legacy format.

This deck focuses on using low-cost spells and creatures to quickly establish board control and tempo advantage over the opponent. By utilizing efficient card draw and disruptive spells like Force of Will, the deck aims to continuously apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Tamiyo, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Murktide Regent provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game, while cards like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm help to smooth out draws and ensure consistency. The deck's mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy while also disrupting the opponent's resources.

This deck focuses on efficiently disrupting the opponent's game plan while deploying threats of its own. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Thoughtseize to control the opponent's resources, as well as utilizing powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf to apply pressure, this deck aims to disrupt and outpace opponents in the Legacy format. With a mixture of disruption, card advantage, and powerful threats, this Dimir Tempo deck aims to stay ahead of the game and force opponents to react to its threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing ramp cards such as Green Sun's Zenith and Gaea's Cradle to quickly bring out powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Wight of the Reliquary. It also incorporates disruption elements with cards like Wasteland and Collector Ouphe to control the board and slow down the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to establish board presence and overwhelm the opponent with its large creatures while also disrupting their game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly developing a board presence through aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Ocelot Pride, while also disrupting opponents with cards like Static Prison and Swords to Plowshares. It aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the game through creature removal and land destruction with cards like Wasteland. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provides additional ways to boost the deck's aggro strategy and close out games quickly. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast, efficient threats and disruptive elements.
